items.title: Aminul hopeful of improved sports diplomacy through India series
items.summary: India last toured Bangladesh in 2022 for a three-match ODI series and a two-match Test series.

items.title: Cummins, Hazlewood, Lyon return to face Bangladesh
items.summary: Australia's veterans named in a 13-man squad with Cameron Green and Beau Webster both included

items.title: Australia's Cummins, Hazlewood, Lyon back for Bangladesh Test series
items.summary: Ashes pace hero Michael Neser was left out of the 13-man party for the two-match series in Darwin and Mackay beginning on August 13, with Scott Boland named as the fourth seamer.

items.title: Hridoy pleased with T20I series win despite 'injuries and limited options'
items.summary: Bangladesh were without Mustafizur Rahman and Shoriful Islam for the entire series, and lost Nahid Rana for the decider, which they won by four wickets

items.title: Bangladesh win dramatic finale to clinch T20I series against Zimbabwe
items.summary: Tanzid Hasan anchored the chase with a half-century as Bangladesh won with two balls to spare

items.title: Tanzid hits fifty as Tigers clinch series with dramatic win in third Zimbabwe T20I
items.summary: Bangladesh completed a series comeback as opener Tanzid Tamim struck an unbeaten half-century to guide the Tigers to a four-wicket win over Zimbabwe in the third and final T20I in Bulawayo on Sunday, sealing the three-match series 2-1.

items.title: Zimbabwe opt to bat in series decider
items.summary: Bangladesh were without Nahid Rana, who suffered a side strain on Friday

items.title: Bangladesh eye series win to finish forgettable tour
items.summary: After winning the one-off Test and the ODI series, Zimbabwe have the chance to clinch the T20I series too

items.title: Openers, Mahedi and Rishad help Bangladesh keep series alive
items.summary: Zimbabwe were sloppy in the field and lost wickets in clusters with the bat

items.title: Saifuddin fireworks, Rishad four-fer keep Tigers alive
items.summary: Half-centuries from openers Saif Hassan and Tanzid Tamim, followed by a blistering late cameo from Mohammad Saifuddin and a four-wicket haul from Rishad Hossain, powered Bangladesh to a 34-run victory over Zimbabwe in the second T20I of the three-match series in Bulawayo on Friday.

items.title: Unchanged Zimbabwe put Bangladesh in to bat
items.summary: Bangladesh brought in Rishad Hossain for Nasum Ahmed

items.title: Buoyant Zimbabwe eye series win against depleted Bangladesh
items.summary: Bangladesh have now lost their last five T20Is and need their top order to fire

items.title: Australia internationals unlikely to play much WNCL
items.summary: Australia's series against Bangladesh, New Zealand and the women's Champions Trophy, as well as the WPL likely to keep their best away from domestic cricket

items.title: Muzarabani and Ngarava overwhelm Bangladesh again
items.summary: The two Zimbabwe quicks picked up four wickets each to defend a total of 170
